name: Red light
meaning: At a red light, come to a complete stop at the stop line or, if there is no stop line, before entering the intersection or before reaching the crosswalk. Remain stopped as long as the signal is red, unless turns are allowed.

name: Right turn on red
meaning: You may turn right while the traffic signal displays a red light. Before turning, you must come to a complete stop. Look both ways and yield the right-of-way to pedestrians and other traffic. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles and mopeds.

name: Left turn on red
meaning: You may turn left at a red light if you are on a one-way street and turning left onto another one-way street while the traffic signal displays a red light. Before turning, you must come to a complete stop. Look both ways and yield the right-of-way to pedestrians and other traffic. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds.

name: Red arrow
meaning: A red arrow means you must stop if you intend to move in the direction of the arrow. You may not proceed in the direction of the arrow as long as the red arrow is displayed, unless signs are posted at the intersection that read "Right on Red Arrow After Stop" or "Left on Red Arrow After Stop." Virginia law prohibits right and left turns at red arrow lights. Note: If you are traveling in another state, make sure you know its laws for right and left turns at red and red arrow lights.

name: Flashing red light
meaning: At a flashing red light, come to a complete stop and yield to oncoming vehicles and pedestrians. You may go when the way is clear. At a railroad crossing, you must come to a complete stop even if you don't see a train.

name: Flashing red arrow
meaning: At a flashing red arrow, come to a complete stop, yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection, and proceed in the direction of the arrow when the way is clear.

name: Yellow light or arrow
meaning: A yellow light or arrow are cautions warning that the light is about to change. If you have not entered the intersection, stop; or, if unsafe to stop, cautiously go through it. If you are already in the intersection, go through it cautiously. Do not speed up to beat the light.

name: Flashing yellow light
meaning: A flashing yellow light means slow down and proceed with caution. Flashing yellow lights are at locations with higher-than-normal hazardous conditions.

name: Flashing yellow arrow
meaning: At a flashing yellow arrow, you may turn in the direction of the arrow, if the way is clear. Yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to flashing yellow arrow while a pedestrian is in the intersection, allow the pedestrian to cross

name: Green light or arrow
meaning: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

name: Out of service signals
meaning: When traffic signals are not working because of a power outage or other problem and not displaying any lights, you are required to stop, proceeding through the intersection as though it were an all-way stop. This does not apply if a law enforcement officer or other authorized person is directing traffic at the intersection, or if portable stop signs are in use.

name: Red X
meaning: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

name: Yellow X or Yellow Diagonal Downward Arrow
meaning: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

name: Green Arrow
meaning: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

name: Left-turn Arrow
meaning: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

name: Pedestrian Hybrid Beacons (PHBs)
meaning: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

name: Octagon (Stop)
meaning: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

name: Triangle (Yield)
meaning: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

name: Rectangle (Regulatory or Guide)
meaning: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

name: Diamond (Warning)
meaning: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

name: Pentagon (School Zone/School Crossing)
meaning: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

name: Speed Limit
meaning: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

name: Do Not Enter – Wrong Way
meaning: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

name: One Way
meaning: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

name: No Left Turn
meaning: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

name: No Right Turn
meaning: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

name: No U-Turn
meaning: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

name: No Turn on Red
meaning: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

name: Do Not Pass
meaning: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

name: Left Turn Yield on Green
meaning: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

name: Keep Right
meaning: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

name: Lane Use Control
meaning: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

name: High Occupancy Vehicle
meaning: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

name: Disabled Parking
meaning: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

name: Advisory Speed
meaning: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

name: Stop Ahead/Yield Ahead
meaning: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

name: Signal Ahead
meaning: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

name: No Passing Zone
meaning: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

name: Merge
meaning: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

name: Lane Reduction
meaning: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

name: Divided Highway Begins
meaning: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

name: Divided Highway Ends
meaning: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

name: Slippery When Wet
meaning: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

name: Low Clearance Sign
meaning: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

name: Hill
meaning: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

name: Deer Crossing
meaning: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

name: Horse-Drawn Buggies
meaning: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

name: Tractors and Farm Equipment
meaning: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

name: Pedestrian Crossing
meaning: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

name: Bicycle Crossing/Bike Path
meaning: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

name: School Zone/School Crossing
meaning: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

name: Open Joints
meaning: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

name: Expansion Joints
meaning: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

name: Intersections
meaning: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

name: Y Intersection
meaning: You must bear either right or left ahead.

name: T Intersection
meaning: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

name: Roundabout
meaning: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

name: Right Curve – Side Road
meaning: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

name: Sharp Right Turn
meaning: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

name: Sharp Right and Left Turns
meaning: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

name: Right and Left Curves
meaning: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

name: Right Curve with Safe Speed Indicator
meaning: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

name: Winding Road
meaning: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

name: Low Ground Railroad Crossing
meaning: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

name: Railroad Crossing
meaning: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

name: Railroad Crossbuck
meaning: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

name: Railroad Crossbuck and Flashing Lights
meaning: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

name: Crossbuck, Flashing Lights and Gate
meaning: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

name: Rough Road, Bump, or Uneven Lanes
meaning: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

name: Road Construction Ahead – Detour
meaning: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

name: Flashing Arrow Boards
meaning: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

name: Flaggers
meaning: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

name: Photo Speed Enforcement
meaning: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

name: Traffic Control Devices
meaning: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

name: Message Boards
meaning: You may see portable or permanent message boards along roadways. They provide information about traffic, road, weather or other hazardous conditions. Always obey any directions posted on these message boards. For information about road conditions or road construction in advance of your trip, visit www.virginiadot.org, www.511virginia.org or call 511.
